key_themes: Major binding Glencore offtake agreement for battery-grade lithium hydroxide
Steady Tees Valley Lithium (TVL) project de-risking (FEED study complete, Wates appointment, on-site recovery tech advances, industry engagement)
Positive analyst commentary (Zeus notes momentum, upside, and potential dividends)
Anticipation for near-term financing, additional offtakes, government support, and FID
Ongoing dilution via repeated loan conversions and share issuances amid pre-revenue losses
summary: Alkemy Capital Investments (ALK.L) has net positive momentum after signing a binding multi-year lithium hydroxide offtake with Glencore, completing FEED work, appointing contractors like Wates, advancing recovery technology, and seeing strong UK/EU battery-supply-chain tailwinds. Zeus research highlights project momentum, potential dividends, and upside as the company moves toward FID and financing. The biggest risk is successfully closing a substantial financing package while managing further dilution, execution on first-of-its-kind UK refinery construction, and pre-revenue losses in a volatile lithium market.

recent_posts.highlight: Cluster of material updates expected in April/May including government support/grants, additional offtakes, financing package, and FID; notes UK government backing for battery supply chain with TVL centrally positioned.

Europe racing to localise battery materials; TVL designed for 25ktpa lithium hydroxide (expandable to 100ktpa) with >94% recovery and competitive capex/opex; FEED complete and advancing to FID/financing.
Strong support from Tees Valley Mayor Ben Houchen on Wates appointment and pre-construction start; project moving into delivery mode.

Excellent CEO interview on flagship UK lithium refinery progress ahead of imminent FID and financing; emphasises supply chain security with South American feedstock and Glencore offtake already secured.
